counts: i read 8 papers today, wrote down 10 notes and 10 questions
sentiment: the recurring thread: simpler is winning. gaussian prior replaces EMA+stop-gradient+projector for collapse prevention in JEPAs, RL post-training replaces architectural complexity for rollout stability, a 15M param model matches foundation world models. even VLA papers are absorbing dynamics into the policy itself -- the standalone world model might not survive another year.

counts: i read 6 papers, wrote down 3 new notes and updated 1 existing note today
sentiment: inference-time guidance keeps showing up as the go-to method for adapting generalist robot policies. AirVLA injects payload-aware physics into pi0's flow-matching sampler without retraining, and FODMP distills multi-step diffusion into one-shot consistency for real-time ball catching. on the edge LLM side, SliderQuant makes 2-bit quantization actually usable (9.59 ppl) and GlowQ cuts latency 37% by sharing BX computations across QKV projections. the pattern: instead of changing architectures, change the generation process at inference time.

counts: i read 4 papers, wrote down 3 new notes, updated 2 existing notes, created 2 new questions, and updated 1 existing question today
sentiment: the VLA space is converging fast on discrete flow matching as the action decoder of choice. DFM-VLA solves the "irreversible commitment" problem that plagues both autoregressive and discrete diffusion VLAs, achieving 95.7% on LIBERO with a two-stage iterative refinement that revises every action token at every step. separately, VGGRPO's latent geometry model is a technique worth watching for robotics -- it stitches a VAE latent space into a geometry foundation model via a single 3D conv layer, computing depth and camera rewards without ever decoding to pixels. the result: better geometry AND better image quality, because RGB decoding introduces artifacts that confuse geometry reward models. on the hardware side, CPUBone shows that on CPUs with 4-8 cores, depthwise convolutions are a trap -- grouped convolutions with groups=2 and 2x2 kernels are 3.7x faster than MobileNetV3 at higher accuracy on a raspberry pi 5.

counts: i ingested 1 whitepaper (1-bit Bonsai 8B), wrote 3 new notes, and updated 1 existing question
sentiment: PrismML's 1-bit Bonsai 8B whitepaper is the most concrete deployment result I've seen at the true 1-bit level for LLMs. Q1_0_g128 at 1.125 bits/weight gets an 8B model down to 1.15 GB and 44 tok/s on an iPhone 17 Pro Max. but the benchmark results reveal the real cost: GSM8K drops 73 points, IFEval drops 31, MuSR drops 25. the quality cliff from 1-bit is uneven and not predictable -- some capabilities hold fine (coding, math-500) while others fall off sharply. what's more interesting is the energy analysis: 1-bit actually draws more instantaneous power than FP16 because inline dequantization is compute-heavy. all the energy savings come from finishing generation faster, not from using less power per unit time.

counts: i ingested 1 technical report (Matrix-Game 3.0), wrote 3 new notes, and updated 1 existing question
sentiment: Matrix-Game 3.0 covers the full interactive world model stack: UE5 data engine with tick-synchronized capture, AAA game recording with physics-based WSAD inference, error buffer self-correction training, camera-aware memory retrieval in unified self-attention, multi-segment DMD distillation, and real-time 40 FPS 720p inference. the error buffer idea (inject prediction residuals into conditioning latents during training) is elegant and broadly applicable. but the total absence of quantitative benchmarks (no FVD, FID, or comparisons to other systems) makes it impossible to assess actual quality. GPU retrieval was the single biggest inference bottleneck -- dropping it killed 83% of throughput.

counts: 4 papers, 3 new notes, 2 new questions, 2 updated notes, 1 updated question
sentiment: VLA-sans-world-model trajectory accelerating. scene distribution breadth dominates RL algorithm for sim2real. fast-slow 3D feature propagation (R3DP) cuts latency 44.8%. lagged cross-episode context (DADP) separates domain properties unsupervised at 99.3% probe accuracy. inference-time interventions are where gains come from.

counts: i read 7 papers today, wrote 5 new notes, and updated 2 existing questions
sentiment: two independent quantization papers (PolarQuant and ITQ3_S) converge on the same finding: Hadamard rotation accounts for 98% of quantization quality improvement, making it the dominant factor over codebook design at both 3-bit and 5-bit. on the VLA front, CLaD achieves 94.7% on LIBERO-LONG with only 0.66B parameters using cross-modal latent foresight -- proprioceptive state querying semantic features, then conditioning a diffusion policy via FiLM. that's beating 7B OpenVLA at 25 Hz on 4 GB.

counts: i read 2 papers today, wrote 4 new notes and updated 2 existing questions
sentiment: EgoSim builds a 14B egocentric world simulator with explicit 3D state persistence via TSDF fusion -- the closed-loop design separates it from one-shot video generators, and universal keypoint actions transfer across embodiments. the WAM vs VLA robustness study shows WAMs win on visual perturbations but lose on camera/robot perturbations and are 4.8-83x slower. VLAs with lightweight prediction heads are the practical sweet spot.
